_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use mathematical modelling to solve practical problems with rational numbers and percentages.

_source.w: We are learning to use mathematical modelling to solve practical problems with rational numbers and percentages.
y: Mathematical modelling turns real-world situations into problems we can solve using our number skills.
The modelling process — formulate, solve, interpret, evaluate — develops our ability to think critically and practically.
These skills transfer to budgeting, planning, scientific research and business analysis.
eg: Modelling a shopping budget: calculating total costs with discounts and GST.
Creating a model to compare two mobile phone plans over 12 months.
Solving: 'A tank holds 500 L. It loses 3% of its water per day. How much is left after 5 days?'
Modelling population growth: 'A town of 8000 grows by 2.5% per year. Population after 3 years?'
Evaluating whether a model's prediction is realistic in context.
Comparing model predictions with actual data to assess accuracy.
